verses.1: For the music director; for flute accompaniment. A Psalm of David. Listen to my words, LORD, Consider my sighing.
verses.2: Listen to the sound of my cry for help, my King and my God, For to You I pray.
verses.3: In the morning, LORD, You will hear my voice; In the morning I will present my prayer to You and be on the watch.
verses.4: For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness; No evil can dwell with You.
verses.5: The boastful will not stand before Your eyes; You hate all who do injustice.
verses.6: You destroy those who speak lies; The LORD loathes the person of bloodshed and deceit.
verses.7: But as for me, by Your abundant graciousness I will enter Your house, At Your holy temple I will bow in reverence for You.
verses.8: LORD, lead me in Your righteousness because of my enemies; Make Your way straight before me.
verses.9: For there is nothing trustworthy in their mouth; Their inward part is destruction itself. Their throat is an open grave; They flatter with their tongue.
verses.10: Make them pay, God; Have them fall by their own schemes! Scatter them in the multitude of their wrongdoings, For they are rebellious against You.
verses.11: But rejoice, all who take refuge in You, Sing for joy forever! And may You shelter them, That those who love Your name may rejoice in You.
verses.12: For You bless the righteous person, LORD, You surround him with favor as with a shield.
